what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon that combines a bracket-like measurement outline with an m² text label.
observations: The SVG uses a 0 0 24 24 viewBox with explicit 24px width and height.
The root sets fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", round line caps, and round joins.
A single path draws horizontal and vertical measurement-like strokes from the left side toward x=15, with small diagonal end marks.
A text element at x="17" y="12" renders "m²" in DM Sans or sans-serif at 6px and font-weight 700.
The text disables stroke and uses fill="currentColor".
standouts: The icon mixes stroked geometric linework with an SVG text glyph.
The m² label is encoded as visible text rather than converted to paths.
conventions: Uses currentColor so the icon can inherit color from its context.
Uses a 24x24 icon viewport and compact inline SVG structure.
Combines path-based icon geometry with typographic content.
concerns: The rendered m² label depends on font availability and SVG text rendering behavior.
The superscript character may render with minor visual differences across environments.
notes: This file is a square-meter icon built from a measurement-style outline and an m² text label.
questions: Should the m² text remain live SVG text or be converted to paths for rendering consistency?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies an m2 or square-meter icon, and the file renders an m²-labeled measurement icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to visually represent square meters by pairing a measurement bracket motif with an m² unit label.
novelty: The distinct feature is the direct SVG text use of the m² unit inside an otherwise stroke-based icon.
contribution: Contributes a compact square-meter unit glyph that preserves inherited coloring and combines measurement linework with an explicit m² label.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 SVG square-meter icon with currentColor stroked measurement lines and a live text m² label.
intention_for_embedding: This icon was for representing an area or square-meter measurement in a design system asset set.
